static_checks.targeted_tests: 6738 passed / 0 failed (7 skipped) across 359 test files — independently re-run via `npm run test:unit -- tests/unit/hooks/useSplitMessages.test.ts tests/unit/lib/terminal-highlight.test.ts tests/unit/components/HistoryPane.test.tsx tests/unit/components/worktree/TerminalSplitPaneContent.test.tsx tests/unit/components/worktree/TerminalContainer.test.tsx tests/unit/components/WorktreeDetailRefactored.test.tsx` (vitest ran the full suite). build also re-run: exit 0, 'Compiled successfully'.

criteria_results.criterion: Per-split independent fetch uses each split's own cliToolId (not state.messages)
criteria_results.evidence: src/hooks/useSplitMessages.ts:79-88 builds `?cliTool=<requestedCli>` (plus limit/includeArchived) and fetches `/api/worktrees/${worktreeId}/messages`. src/components/worktree/TerminalSplitPaneContent.tsx:179-189 calls useSplitMessages({worktreeId, cliToolId, ...}) with the split's own cliToolId and feeds `splitMessages` into the embedded HistoryPane (L294-296). Tests: tests/unit/hooks/useSplitMessages.test.ts:62-101 assert per-cliToolId URL + params; tests/unit/components/worktree/TerminalSplitPaneContent.test.tsx:643-680 assert two splits (claude/codex) render their own message counts simultaneously.
criteria_results.notes: state.messages (parent, activeCliTab-filtered) is NOT used by the PC split history; each split owns its fetch.

criteria_results.criterion: Namespace isolation: makeHistoryNamespace(0) vs (1) differ; one split's apply/clear does not affect another; legacy HISTORY_SEARCH_NAMESPACE preserved; CSS rules exist for -0/1/2 and -current-0/1/2
criteria_results.evidence: src/lib/terminal-highlight.ts:79-86 makeHistoryNamespace(idx) -> `history-search-${idx}` / `history-search-current-${idx}` / `history-search-fallback-overlay-${idx}`. applyHistoryHighlights/clearHistoryHighlights (L285-313) default to HISTORY_SEARCH_NAMESPACE when no namespace passed (backward compat). HistoryPane.tsx:219-225 selects makeHistoryNamespace(splitIndex) only when splitIndex !== undefined, else legacy. globals.css:167-179 declares `::highlight(history-search-0|1|2)` and `::highlight(history-search-current-0|1|2)` (legacy history-search rules retained L142-156). Tests: tests/unit/lib/terminal-highlight.test.ts:374-419 prove split-0 apply does not delete split-1 registry key and clearing split-0 does not touch split-1; L426-459 prove no-arg calls still use legacy `history-search`.
criteria_results.notes: Static CSS rules cover MAX_SPLITS=3 exactly (terminal-split-config). 4th+ split would have no CSS rule, but MAX_SPLITS=3 enforces the bound.

criteria_results.criterion: Backward compatibility: HistoryPane with no splitIndex/cliToolId uses legacy namespace; mobile path unchanged
criteria_results.evidence: HistoryPane.tsx:219-225 legacy-namespace fallback. tests/unit/components/HistoryPane.test.tsx:638-656 assert no per-split namespace object is passed when splitIndex omitted; L658-671 assert history-search-1 when splitIndex=1. Mobile unchanged: `git diff HEAD` of WorktreeDetailRefactored.tsx shows the #744 working-tree changes touch only the desktop branch (renderSplitPane history wiring + removal of top-level historyPaneMemo/TerminalContainer history prop + handleInsertToSplit). The mobile region (MobileContent props at ~L2012 and bottom MessageInput at ~L2066, and the mobile CLI tab bar L1944-1974) is NOT in the #744 diff (those `cliToolId={activeCliTab}` / status-indicator changes predate #744, from #736/#743).
criteria_results.notes: Verified the actual #744 delta via `git diff HEAD` (uncommitted working tree) rather than `git diff main` (which also contains #723-#743). Mobile rendering is genuinely untouched by this iteration.

criteria_results.criterion: Insert routing via explicit splitIndex (not focusedSplitIndex indirection)
criteria_results.evidence: WorktreeDetailRefactored.tsx renderSplitPane passes `onHistoryInsertToMessage={(text) => handleInsertToSplit(splitIndex, text)}` (HEAD diff, embedded-HistoryPane wiring block). handleInsertToSplit (WorktreeDetailRefactored.tsx:390-396 region) sets pendingInsertTextMap.set(splitIndex, text) directly — independent of focusedSplitIndex (which is only used by the memo/Notes-pane generic handleInsertToMessage). TerminalSplitPaneContent.tsx:301 forwards onHistoryInsertToMessage to HistoryPane.onInsertToMessage. Test tests/unit/components/worktree/TerminalSplitPaneContent.test.tsx:682-705 asserts the split-bound handler fires with the inserted content.
criteria_results.notes: Per-split insert targets its own MessageInput, not the last-focused split.

criteria_results.criterion: stale-guard (requestId) + visibilitychange pause + refresh() in useSplitMessages
criteria_results.evidence: src/hooks/useSplitMessages.ts:69-111 requestIdRef + inFlightCliToolRef drop superseded/wrong-CLI responses (L92-97, L101-105). L131-134 interval skips when document.visibilityState==='hidden'; L141-146 re-fetch on visibilitychange->visible. L155 refresh(). L117-124 (worktreeId,cliToolId)-keyed reset bumps requestId + clears messages. Tests cover all: useSplitMessages.test.ts:134-167 (stale drop), 169-176 (enabled=false), 178-194 (hidden pause), 196-214 (refresh), 216-234 (reset on change).

criteria_results.criterion: Each PC split renders its own independent HistoryPane (1-3 splits)
criteria_results.evidence: TerminalSplitPaneContent.tsx:292-330 historyPaneSlot + L355-418 [History|Resizer|Terminal] layout with split-scoped testids `split-history-slot-${splitIndex}` (L361) and collapse `split-history-expand-${splitIndex}` (L381). Test TerminalSplitPaneContent.test.tsx:602-620 'renders a HistoryPane inside the split'.

criteria_results.criterion: Existing HistoryPane features (search #716 / User-only #725 / display-limit #701) work per-split
criteria_results.evidence: Search is per-split (HistoryPane internal state + per-split highlight namespace). limit/includeArchived flow into each split's useSplitMessages fetch (TerminalSplitPaneContent.tsx:186-188); display-limit/user-only/show-archived flow into each HistoryPane (L300-307). Common (not per-split-independent) values by MVP design.
criteria_results.notes: MVP: these toggles are shared across splits (single source from parent), documented as a follow-up in tdd-result. Functionally per-split rendering works.

criteria_results.criterion: Worktree switch keeps each CLI's history correct
criteria_results.evidence: useSplitMessages.ts:115-124 resets + re-fetches on (worktreeId,cliToolId) change; test useSplitMessages.test.ts:216-234.

criteria_results.criterion: A=Claude / B=Codex show only their own CLI messages simultaneously
criteria_results.evidence: Each split fetches independently by its own cliToolId. Test TerminalSplitPaneContent.test.tsx:643-680 mounts two TerminalSplitPaneContent (claude split 0, codex split 1) and asserts message-count 1 vs 2 with distinct data-split-index 0/1.

criteria_results.criterion: A=Claude / B=Claude show both Claude messages
criteria_results.evidence: Code path is correct: each split keys useSplitMessages by its own cliToolId, so two claude splits would both fetch claude and render identical messages, with namespaces still separated by splitIndex (HistoryPane.tsx:219-225 keys on splitIndex, NOT cliToolId). HOWEVER this scenario is UNREACHABLE through the normal split UI: useTerminalSplits.setSplitCliTool (src/hooks/useTerminalSplits.ts:175-181) forbids same-CLI-across-splits (S1-002) and availableCliTools (L192-201) filters out already-used CLIs from each split's selector. No dedicated unit test mounts two same-cliToolId TerminalSplitPaneContent.
criteria_results.notes: Marked partial because the criterion as written cannot occur via the product UI (S1-002 from #728). The per-split history code would handle it correctly if it ever did. Low-severity gap; recommend either documenting the AC as N/A given S1-002, or adding a same-CLI mount test purely to lock the namespace-by-splitIndex guarantee.

criteria_results.evidence: `git diff HEAD` shows zero changes to the mobile render path in this iteration (see backward-compat criterion). MobileContent still receives messages={state.messages} + cliToolId={activeCliTab} (unchanged from #736).

criteria_results.criterion: Existing tests PASS (splitIndex/cliToolId optional => backward compatible)
criteria_results.evidence: Full suite 6738 passed / 0 failed on my independent re-run. Only existing-test change: WorktreeDetailRefactored.test.tsx TC-4 recovery-throttle filter now also excludes `/messages` (git diff HEAD shows a 1-line additive filter, mirroring the pre-existing `/current-output` exclusion). This is a legitimate test-harness update for independent per-split polling, not a masking of a regression.

criteria_results.criterion: New tests added (per-split filtering / namespace isolation / useSplitMessages)
criteria_results.evidence: tests/unit/hooks/useSplitMessages.test.ts (10 tests), tests/unit/lib/terminal-highlight.test.ts (+#744 makeHistoryNamespace isolation + backward-compat blocks L335-460), tests/unit/components/HistoryPane.test.tsx (+#744 per-split namespace block L614-690), tests/unit/components/worktree/TerminalSplitPaneContent.test.tsx (+#744 embedded HistoryPane block L593-722), tests/unit/components/worktree/TerminalContainer.test.tsx (history-omitted render + no expand button).

criteria_results.criterion: npm run lint / npx tsc --noEmit / npm run test:unit / npm run build all PASS
criteria_results.evidence: Independently executed: tsc exit 0; lint exit 0 ('No ESLint warnings or errors'); vitest 6738 passed/0 failed; next build exit 0 ('Compiled successfully', full route table incl. /worktrees/[id]).

adversarial_findings.finding: Duplicate data-testid="history-pane-collapse-button" and dangling aria-controls="worktree-history-pane" across PC splits. Each split's HistoryPane is rendered with onCollapse set (TerminalSplitPaneContent.tsx:308), so the collapse button renders in every split with hardcoded id references (HistoryPane.tsx:531 aria-controls + L534 data-testid). With 2-3 splits the DOM contains 2-3 identical testids, and aria-controls points at HISTORY_PANE_ID which is NOT rendered on PC (TerminalContainer only emits that id when given a `history` prop, which it is not on PC).
adversarial_findings.impact: No functional break (collapse works via onClick; split-scoped testids `split-history-expand-${idx}` exist for e2e). But: (a) any future getByTestId('history-pane-collapse-button') would throw on the multi-split DOM; (b) screen readers cannot resolve the aria-controls target region (a11y nit). Current unit tests render a single HistoryPane so they don't surface the collision.
adversarial_findings.recommendation: Either suffix the collapse button id/testid by splitIndex (e.g. accept a splitIndex-derived testid + aria-controls pointing at the split's own slot id `split-history-slot-${idx}`), or omit onCollapse for the split-embedded HistoryPane since the split already provides its own expand/collapse bar (`split-history-expand-${idx}`). Low priority.

adversarial_findings.finding: Acceptance criterion 'A=Claude / B=Claude' is unreachable via the product UI. useTerminalSplits (S1-002, #728) forbids the same CLI in two splits (setSplitCliTool no-ops, availableCliTools filters used CLIs).
adversarial_findings.impact: The AC as written cannot be exercised by a user. The per-split code is correct (namespace keyed by splitIndex, fetch keyed by cliToolId) so there is no defect, but the AC gives false assurance of a tested scenario that the container actively prevents.
adversarial_findings.recommendation: Document the AC as N/A given S1-002, or add a direct unit test that mounts two TerminalSplitPaneContent with the same cliToolId but different splitIndex to lock the 'namespace keyed by splitIndex (not cliToolId)' invariant independently of the container guard.

adversarial_findings.finding: Redundant /messages fetch on PC. The parent adaptive poll (WorktreeDetailRefactored.tsx:1384-1391 pollData) still calls fetchMessages() to populate state.messages every interval, but on PC state.messages is no longer rendered (top-level HistoryPane removed) — it is only consumed by the mobile MobileContent. So on PC the active CLI is fetched twice: once by the parent (unused result) and once by the split's useSplitMessages.
adversarial_findings.impact: Minor wasted network/CPU on PC (one extra /messages request per poll interval for activeCliTab). No correctness issue. Acknowledged implicitly in useSplitMessages.ts header comment (state.messages is activeCliTab-only) but the now-unused PC fetch is not gated.
adversarial_findings.recommendation: Optional follow-up: gate the parent fetchMessages() to mobile-only (e.g. `if (isMobile)`), or stop populating state.messages on PC. Out of scope for the per-split feature; safe to defer.

adversarial_findings.finding: Per-split History collapsed/visible state and width are shared across splits (single useHistoryPaneState backing one localStorage key), so collapsing one split's history collapses all, and resizing applies the same percent to each split's inner area.
adversarial_findings.impact: UX deviation from a strict 'per-split independent' reading, but explicitly an MVP decision (tdd-result follow-up S3-004; TerminalSplitPaneContent.tsx:191-196 comment). The width is at least applied relative to each split's own inner area (handleHistoryResize uses the split container offsetWidth), so layout is not globally distorted.
adversarial_findings.recommendation: Accept for MVP; revisit per-split visible/width if UX feedback requires. No action needed for this iteration.

summary: Issue #744 independently verified as PASSED WITH NOTES. All four static checks were re-run from scratch and pass: tsc exit 0, ESLint exit 0 ('No ESLint warnings or errors'), full vitest suite 6738 passed / 0 failed / 7 skipped (359 files), next build exit 0 ('Compiled successfully'). Code-read verification confirms the core design: useSplitMessages fetches /messages?cliTool=<own cliToolId> per split with a proper requestId+inFlightCli stale-guard, visibilitychange pause, refresh(), and (worktreeId,cliToolId)-keyed reset; makeHistoryNamespace(splitIndex) yields history-search-0/1/2 with isolation proven by tests (split-0 apply/clear does not touch split-1) and matching static CSS rules in globals.css; HistoryPane falls back to the legacy HISTORY_SEARCH_NAMESPACE when splitIndex is omitted (backward compat verified by test). Insert routing is by explicit splitIndex (handleInsertToSplit), not focusedSplitIndex. Adversarial checks: TerminalContainer still compiles/renders with history omitted; no dangling historyPaneMemo references (only a comment); HISTORY_PANE_ID is single-instance and not rendered on PC (no duplicate id from that source); namespace is keyed by splitIndex (not cliToolId) so same-CLI splits cannot collide; no e2e references the top-level history-pane-expand. Mobile path is genuinely unchanged in this iteration (confirmed via git diff HEAD, which isolates the #744 working-tree delta from the stacked #723-#743 history in git diff main). Notable gaps (all low severity, none blocking): (1) the embedded split HistoryPane renders a hardcoded collapse button with duplicate data-testid='history-pane-collapse-button' and a dangling aria-controls='worktree-history-pane' across splits — a11y/test-robustness nit; (2) the 'A=Claude / B=Claude' acceptance criterion is unreachable because useTerminalSplits S1-002 forbids same-CLI splits (code is correct but the scenario can't occur, and no test locks the splitIndex-keyed namespace invariant for same-CLI); (3) the parent poll still fetches /messages for the now-PC-unused state.messages, double-fetching the active CLI; (4) per-split History visible/width are shared (documented MVP). Recommend addressing finding (1) (suffix the collapse testid/aria-controls by splitIndex or drop the redundant onCollapse) and clarifying AC for finding (2) in a follow-up.
